title: Pan Seared, Oven Roasted Thick-cut Pork Chops with Spicy Tomato Pan Sauce
ingredients: ¾ cup packed light brown sugar
4 pork chops, 1½ inches thick (about 12 ounces each)
½ tsp dried crushed red pepper
1 cup Kalamata olives, pitted and coarsely chopped
instructions: Dissolve brown sugar and salt in 6 cups cold water.
Place brine in a gallon-size plastic bag and add pork chops, then seal the bag, pressing out as much air as possible.
Remove the chops from the brine, rinse, and pat thoroughly dry with paper towels.
Season chops with the ground black pepper.
Adjust oven rack to the lower middle position and place a shallow roasting pan or rimmed baking pan on the rack.
When the oven reaches 450 degrees F, heat the oil in a heavy skillet over high heat.
Lay the chops in the skillet and cook until well browned with a nice crust on the surface, about 3 minutes.
Turn the chops over and cook until well browned with a nice crust on the other side.
Using tongs, transfer the chops to the preheated pan in the oven.
Roast until the internal temperature reaches 130 degrees F, about 12-15 minutes.
Transfer chops to a platter, tent loosely with foil, and let rest for 5 minutes (temperature should rise to 145 degrees F).
Add red pepper and parsley to the same skillet that the pork was cooked in and sauté for 1 minute, scraping up browned bits from the skillet.
Add tomatoes, olives, and garlic, and sauté for 2-3 minutes.
Season the sauce with salt and pepper to taste.
Place pork chops on a serving platter and spoon Spicy Tomato Pan Sauce over the top.
notes: The pork chops should reach an internal temperature of 145 degrees F when fully cooked; resting allows the temperature to rise from 130 to 145 degrees F.
Make sure to pat the pork chops completely dry after brining to ensure a proper crust forms during searing.
The sauce is made in the same skillet as the pork to capture all the flavorful browned bits.
